headline: Market rebounds on de-escalation signs; AVN still lagging near the sell zone
market: The geopolitical selloff is reversing: KSE-100 has now rallied over 1,700 points in back-to-back sessions (Jul 9–10) to ~183,250, as oil prices ease and global markets read the Iran-Israel situation as de-escalating (though the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to tanker traffic and the ceasefire is still fragile). The index is back to within ~1.9% of its recent peak (~186,800) — nowhere near the 10–15% correction that would justify deploying dry powder. SBP held its policy rate at 11.5% on Jun 15 despite May inflation running hot at 11.7%; the IMF completed its 3rd EFF/2nd RSF review in May with $4.8B disbursed to date and reserves near $16–17B — the macro backdrop remains supportive. Your portfolio: ~Rs 10.22M value on ~Rs 9.49M invested (+7.56% unrealized), up slightly from +7.36% three days ago as most holdings recovered with the index. Laggards: AVN (still −23%, unmoved by the rally — a relative-weakness signal, not just market noise), CPHL (−7.5%, digesting the REIT demerger news) and MZNPETF (−7.6%, still working off its highs).

actions.title: Avanceon — fundamentals broken, price flat even as the broader market rallies
actions.detail: Your largest stock position (Rs 1.1M in) and worst performer (−23%). FY25 net income fell 68%, Q3 revenue fell 44%, margins collapsed from 12% to 4%, and 3-year shareholder return is −32%. Do not average down. Price is Rs 34.67 today — essentially unchanged from Rs 34.61 last review even as KSE-100 rallied 1,700+ points over two sessions. That's a relative-weakness signal on top of the broken fundamentals: no company-specific news, no catalyst until the Sep 2, 2026 earnings report. Don't wait for a bounce back to 36–38 — sell half at market now and lock in the decision; exit the rest fully if Sep 2 earnings show no recovery.
actions.target: Sell ~12,000 shares now near Rs 34–35, split proceeds ~60% into MEBL / ~40% into MIIETF

actions.title: Meezan Bank — core compounder
actions.detail: +53% and Pakistan's premier Islamic bank with structural deposit advantage. This is exactly the 20-year stock you want. Add on any 10%+ dip.

actions.title: Faysal Bank — Islamic conversion paying off
actions.detail: +50% since purchase. Strong deposit growth post Islamic conversion, good dividends. Long-term keep.

actions.title: Fatima Fertilizer
actions.detail: +52% with concessionary-gas cost advantage and solid dividends. Keep; it funds itself.

actions.title: Engro Fertilizers — dividend cut, inventory glut
actions.detail: Flat on cost, ~7% dividend yield, but Q1 2026 urea offtake fell 73% QoQ and EFERT holds 48% of industry inventory; payout was trimmed to 80%. Discounts were fully withdrawn from Apr 4, 2026 (market share down to ~27%), and new CEO Imran Ahmed joined May 11 — no results yet on his tenure. It's an income stock, not a grower. Keep for yield but if H1 results show continued weak offtake, rotate into MEBL or an ETF.
actions.target: Re-evaluate after H1 2026 results (~Aug); if weak, sell and split proceeds toward MEBL/MZNPETF

actions.title: Mughal Iron & Steel — cyclical winner
actions.detail: +36%. Steel is cyclical; enjoy the construction upcycle but be ready to trim if it doubles from cost. Not a forever stock.

actions.title: Citi Pharma — fine, just re-rating, plus a REIT demerger in motion
actions.detail: −7% (mostly the broad Jul 8 selloff) but fundamentals are intact: FY25 revenue +6%, earnings +7%, ~4% dividend yield, low volatility (beta 0.51). Board approved a REIT demerger on Jul 8 — two Lahore properties (4.1 and 27.15 kanal) move into a new SPV, Citi Core Holdings, in exchange for shares issued back to CPHL. Potential long-term value-unlock from carving out real estate; too early to size the impact. No reason to sell; also no urgency to add at ~20x forward earnings.

actions.title: Meezan Pakistan ETF — your best vehicle for new money
actions.detail: −6% is entry-timing, nothing more. For a 10–25 year horizon, broad Shariah-compliant index exposure is where fresh contributions and dividends should go. Averages out single-stock risk like AVN.

actions.title: Mahaana Islamic Index ETF
actions.detail: Same logic as MZNPETF — low-cost index compounding. Add regularly.

actions.title: Air Link — high beta, policy-dependent
actions.detail: +7%. Mobile-assembly economics depend on import/tax policy. Fine to hold, but cap position size and trim on sharp rallies.

actions.title: Fauji Foods — turnaround, keep small
actions.detail: +18% and newly profitable, but still a turnaround story. Keep the position small; take profits if it runs 50%+.

actions.title: Bestway Cement
actions.detail: +11%. Cement upcycle with infrastructure spending; dividend payer. Hold.

actions.title: National Foods
actions.detail: +14%. Consumer staple compounder — hold for decades.

actions.title: GlaxoSmithKline Pakistan
actions.detail: +9%. Defensive pharma with drug-pricing deregulation tailwind. Hold.

actions.title: Searle
actions.detail: +16%. Pharma margin recovery underway post-deregulation. Hold.

actions.title: Ghani Glass
actions.detail: +18%. Solid industrial. Hold.

actions.title: Dolmen City REIT
actions.detail: +26% plus steady rental dividends. Good inflation hedge; hold.

actions.title: Unilever Pakistan Foods
actions.detail: +22% but only 3 shares (~Rs 77K). Exceptional company, illiquid stock. Hold; immaterial to portfolio decisions.

actions.title: Al-Ameen Shariah Stock Fund
actions.detail: +15% — your largest fund. Actively managed Islamic equity; solid long-term vehicle. Hold and let it compound.

actions.title: Meezan Islamic Fund
actions.detail: +20%. Flagship Islamic equity fund. Hold.

actions.title: NBP Islamic Stock Fund
actions.detail: +25%. Hold.

actions.title: AKD Islamic Stock Fund
actions.detail: +7%. Hold.

actions.title: NBP Islamic Sarmaya Izafa — balanced fund
actions.detail: +5%. Balanced allocation gives smoother rides; fine to keep as ballast.

actions.title: Al-Ameen Islamic Cash Fund — dry powder
actions.detail: Rs ~200K parked at money-market rates. With the index at record highs this is useful dry powder: deploy into MZNPETF/equity funds on a 10–15% market correction.

actions.title: Mahaana Islamic Cash Fund — dry powder
actions.detail: Same as AICF — this is your correction-buying reserve.

actions.title: Dust position (~Rs 2,300)
actions.detail: Too small to matter. Redeem into a main fund next time you transact, just to declutter.

actions.title: Dust position (~Rs 235)
actions.detail: Same — consolidate away.

allocation.rationale: With the index at record highs after a 44% year, new money goes mostly into broad Islamic index ETFs (diversified, no single-stock risk), a Meezan Bank slice only if it dips below Rs 500, an actively managed fund leg, and a reserve slice into your cash fund as dry powder for the next correction.

items.name: Meezan Pakistan ETF
items.note: Broad index exposure — the default home for new money

items.name: Meezan Bank
items.note: Only below Rs 500 — otherwise this slice goes to the others

items.name: Al-Ameen Islamic Cash Fund
items.note: Dry powder — deploy on a 10–15% market correction

notes: Big picture for a 10–25 year horizon: (1) Your only real problem is AVN — a large position with deteriorating fundamentals; fix that first. (2) Direct new savings into the ETFs and equity funds rather than adding more single-stock names — you already hold 18 stocks, which is plenty. (3) Keep the ~Rs 400K in cash funds as correction ammunition. (4) Reinvest all dividends. (5) Expect and ignore 10–15% drawdowns; they are the price of the 40%+ years.
disclaimer: Automated research by your Claude portfolio agent (last reviewed 10 Jul 2026) — a decision aid, not licensed financial advice. Prices/NAVs may lag. Verify with your broker before trading.
